project: Thesis Revision: Fixing the Grand Synthesis Framework
goal: Systematically repair the core technical flaws while preserving the architectural vision. Produce a revised, academically sound thesis.
root_prompt: You are an expert number theorist and category theorist. Revise the provided thesis sections to fix all identified errors with full rigour. Maintain originality but adopt honest, precise academic tone. Output revised LaTeX/HTML sections + detailed change log.

tree.title: Resolve the Verlinde Vacuum-Channel Projector and Str_MTC Identity
tree.prompt: Re-derive or redefine the Verlinde vacuum-channel projector Π₀ (and possibly the universal category C_∞) so that the graded supertrace Str_MTC(Π₀ W^n) behaves as needed. Provide: (a) precise mathematical definition with weighted completions, (b) full proof or rigorous justification of the trace identity (or corrected version with explicit remainder), (c) numerical verification code using mpmath/SymPy for k=2..20 and n=1..10. If exact identity is impossible, propose a renormalised projector or alternative MTC that achieves exact cancellation of non-vacuum channels.

children.prompt: Propose 2–3 concrete alternatives (e.g., channel-by-channel RT projector, large-k limit, twisted weights). Prove boundedness of Π₀ and W in the weighted ℓ² completion (λ_k = 2^{-k}).
Update Appendix A code: remove hard-coded Pi0_factor=1; implement the new projector; show local supertrace values converge appropriately.

tree.title: Repair Local Superdeterminant and Related Claims
tree.prompt: Using the fixed projector from Node 1, prove the corrected local identity: sdet(1 - L_{p,s}) = (1 - p^{-s}) · R_p(s), where R_p(s) is the explicit MTC correction factor. Revise Chapters 2–6 accordingly. Include full operator-norm proofs on the weighted Hilbert spaces.

children.prompt: Replace Theorem A with a rigorous statement about the regularised infinite product over levels. Prove convergence.
Derive the explicit universal ribbon correction R_p(s) (no longer identically 1).

tree.title: Update Adelic Gluing and Functional Equation
tree.prompt: Revise Chapter 8: construct the adelic superdeterminant using the corrected local factors. Prove the global identity equals a deformed completed L-function (or retain exact match if possible). Show functional equation holds for the corrected object.

tree.title: Rebuild Dirac Operator and Determinant Identity
tree.prompt: Rewrite Chapters 10–11: define D rigorously on finite-depth truncations and dense subalgebra A₀ first. Prove spectral triple axioms there. State the determinant identity det_ζ(1−sD⁻¹) = corrected_ζ̂(s)⁻¹ only on A₀ and mark global extension as conjecture.

children.prompt: Strengthen Nelson/Kato–Rellich arguments using the corrected uniform bounds from the new projector.

tree.title: Rewrite Chapter 14 & 15 Honestly
tree.prompt: Revert Chapter 15 'resolutions' to honest conjectural status or partial results. Update all four obstructions with the new corrected framework. Remove all '100% resolved' and 'RH holds' claims.

tree.title: Revise Appendix A and Supporting Evidence
tree.prompt: Update the full computational backbone: implement corrected projector, add error plots vs depth, change all language from 'exact' to 'approximates within truncation error'. Verify first 5–10 zeros only as heuristic support.

tree.title: Global Tone, Abstract, and Framing
tree.prompt: Revise Abstract, Introduction, Epilogue, and Declaration of Rigour. Adopt precise language: 'proposes a framework', 'conjecturally realises', 'heuristic pathway toward Hilbert–Pólya'. Move strong claims to a new 'Speculative Outlook' section.

final_output_prompt: After completing all nodes, compile the full revised thesis HTML/LaTeX. Provide: (1) complete change-log JSON, (2) summary of remaining open conjectures, (3) suggested journal targets.
usage_instruction: Execute nodes sequentially. For each node, feed the prompt + relevant original thesis sections into Grok (or equivalent) and iterate until the output satisfies rigour checks.
